content:
- Finished task 06 execution: added a narrow runner seam proving config-only connection strings and typed verify invocation inputs, replaced duplicate request filter traversal with `JobRequest.Compile() -> RunRequest`, and hardened HTTP tests for hostile filters, hostile job IDs, hostile result strings, and ignored connection-like request fields.
- Verification completed: `cd cockroachdb_molt/molt && GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./verifyservice`, plus root `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed. A broader `go test ./...` in the vendored Go slice still has unrelated environment-dependent failures outside `verifyservice`.
- Task file and execution plan are marked complete/passing, and `.ralph/task_switch.sh` has been run. Next immediate step is only to commit all changes and push.

- Read previous progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, read choose-task rules, and inspected the active backlog plus done backlog.
- Confirmed the actual stack is a Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`, `crates/ingest-contract`) with a vendored Go verify HTTP service under `cockroachdb_molt/molt`; `Makefile` runs cargo-based check/lint/test.
- Resolved visible blocker state from task files: no active failing bugs, no active priority-tagged tasks, and `story-22a/02` is blocked by `story-22a/01`.
- Most logical next task is story 18 task 07 because story 18 task 06 was just completed, and task 07 continues the same verify-image architecture seam by forcing all correctness testing through the production verify HTTP image path before metrics/audit/pruning work.
- Next immediate step after this context window is to keep `.ralph/current_task.txt` pointed at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/07-task-route-all-correctness-tests-through-the-verify-http-image-only.md` and stop.

- Wrote the next selected task to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Selected path: `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/07-task-route-all-correctness-tests-through-the-verify-http-image-only.md`.
- Stop here; the next worker should execute story 18 task 07 and then re-run `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read prior progress, confirmed the selected task is story 18 task 07, and loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Next I am reading the task file plus any task-local plan artifacts to determine whether this turn must stop after planning or can execute.

- Determined this turn is planning-only because story 18 task 07 has no plan artifact yet.
- Inspected the current verify-service HTTP/runtime boundary plus runner E2E harnesses. The active correctness path in Rust tests still proves convergence by direct destination/helper SQL snapshots instead of routing through the published verify image HTTP API.
- Next I need to write a concrete plan that introduces one typed correctness-audit boundary backed by the verify image container, deletes bypass helpers from the shared harnesses, and adds enforcement contract tests so future test code cannot reintroduce direct verify shortcuts.

- Created the first plan artifact for story 18 task 07 at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/07-task-route-all-correctness-tests-through-the-verify-http-image-only_plans/2026-04-19-route-correctness-through-verify-http-image-plan.md` and linked it from the task file.
- The plan is intentionally left at `TO BE VERIFIED`, not `NOW EXECUTE`, because the current shared Rust E2E environment still uses insecure Cockroach plus non-TLS Postgres while the supported verify-service config requires TLS-backed DB connections.
- Next worker should resolve or further design the honest DB-connectivity path for the verify image first; only if that first tracer slice is sound should the plan move to `NOW EXECUTE` and implementation begin.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/07-task-route-all-correctness-tests-through-the-verify-http-image-only.md`.
- Next I am reading the task file and its referenced plan to determine whether this turn must remain planning-only or can execute.

- Read the task file and the existing plan artifact.
- Confirmed the plan currently ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`; the blocking design question is whether the verify image can connect honestly to the same DBs as the Rust E2E harness without adding insecure/test-only compatibility paths.
- Next I am reading the current Rust harness and Go verify-service config/runtime files to settle whether the plan can move to `NOW EXECUTE` this turn.

- Reviewed the existing story-18 task-07 plan against the real harness/runtime code instead of executing.
- Resolved the previous open design question: the execution path should upgrade the shared fixture to secure Cockroach plus SSL-capable Postgres, keep verify-service config strict, and route only the verify image through those TLS-backed DB URLs while keeping one shared source/destination state.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/07-task-route-all-correctness-tests-through-the-verify-http-image-only_plans/2026-04-19-route-correctness-through-verify-http-image-plan.md` to replace the unresolved section with the concrete execution design and changed the footer from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Per task workflow, I am stopping immediately after promoting the plan.
- Next worker should read the task file, see the linked plan marked `NOW EXECUTE`, then execute the plan with red-green TDD starting from the honest verify-connectivity tracer slice.

- Read story-18 task 07, its linked plan, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed execution mode is active because the plan footer is `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Inspected the current boundaries: `VerifyImageHarness` only covers packaging, while `CdcE2eHarness` and scenario harnesses still prove correctness through public destination snapshot helpers.
- Validated the plan's TLS premise with shell probes instead of guessing.
- Secure Cockroach is feasible for the shared fixture by generating CA/node/client certs, keeping `--listen-addr=localhost:26257`, and exposing SQL on a separate secure listener via `--sql-addr=0.0.0.0:26258` plus `--advertise-sql-addr=cockroach:26258`.
- SSL Postgres is also feasible without changing the runner contract by starting the normal container, installing a CA-signed server cert into `PGDATA`, enabling `ssl=on`, restarting once, and then connecting from both host and sibling containers with `sslmode=verify-ca`.
- No product-boundary widening appears necessary so far; next step is to add the first failing verify-image runtime/connectivity contract test and implement the minimal fixture/runtime changes behind it.

- Added the first real verify-image contract test: `verify_image_proves_default_e2e_correctness_through_the_real_http_contract`.
- Implemented the first execution slice behind it instead of widening product config:
  - upgraded the shared E2E fixture to secure Cockroach with generated CA/node/client certs and a dedicated SQL listener on 26258
  - upgraded the shared Postgres fixture to restart with SSL enabled and a generated CA-signed server cert while preserving the runner's non-SSL path
  - extended `VerifyImageHarness` from packaging-only checks into a runtime harness that mounts config/certs, starts the container, probes readiness through `GET /jobs/{missing}`, submits `POST /jobs`, and polls typed job results
  - introduced a first typed `VerifyCorrectnessAudit` boundary in `e2e_integrity.rs`
  - added a narrow `verify_selected_tables_via_image(...)` path through `DefaultBootstrapHarness` and `CdcE2eHarness`
- Verified the tracer slice with `RUSTFLAGS='-Awarnings' cargo test -q -p runner verify_image_proves_default_e2e_correctness_through_the_real_http_contract -- --exact`.
- Next step is to keep executing the plan: enforce the typed audit boundary more cleanly, migrate the default long-lane correctness checkpoints off raw destination snapshot helpers, and then extend the contract tests to block bypass reintroduction.

- resumed execution on task 07 after the first verify-image contract slice was green
- next I am checking remaining long-lane correctness call sites and will reroute them through the verify HTTP image harness
- after code changes I still need to run make check, make lint, and make test before touching Ralph completion state

- migrated default long-lane correctness checks to verify-image-backed positive and mismatch assertions
- started refactoring composite and multi-mapping harnesses onto the same verify HTTP boundary, including secure bootstrap config for multi-mapping
- running focused contract tests now and removing leftover unused snapshot constants/helpers before full repo validation

- focused verify-image contract tests and integrity contracts are green
- compiled the ignored default_bootstrap_long_lane binary successfully after fixing a private-field boundary in multi-mapping support
- starting required repo validation now: make check, make lint, make test

- make check passed
- make lint passed
- make test passed after updating the docker-orchestration allowlist for the new verify image artifact harness
- next I am marking the task passed, running task_switch.sh, committing all tracked changes, and pushing
